# `energy/installed_mw_by_state`

<!-- AUTO-GENERATED by `python -m yen_gov indicator-pages`. Do not hand-edit. -->

**Title**: Installed power-generation capacity (siting basis)  
**One-line**: Nameplate capacity of power plants physically located within each state.  
**Last Updated**: 2026-05-15T20:53:11Z (auto-generated)  
**Source artifact**: [`datasets/indicators/in/energy/installed_mw_by_state.json`](../../../../datasets/indicators/in/energy/installed_mw_by_state.json)

## Definition

Nameplate capacity of power plants physically located within each state. NOT a measure of how much electricity each state consumes — much of the capacity feeds the regional grid and serves multiple states under CEA allocations. Read this map as 'where the plants stand', not 'who uses the power'.

## Signature

| Field | Value |
| --- | --- |
| Entity kind | `state` |
| Time grain | `year` |
| Value kind | `raw` |
| Unit | `MW` |
| Direction | `neutral` |
| Comparability | `not_comparable_across_states` |
| Attribution geography | `where_produced` |
| Implementing authority | `joint` |
| Scale hint | `linear` |

## Coverage

- **Temporal**: 2019 (1 period)
- **Spatial**: 4 of 35 states/UTs (TN, KL, AS, WB) — see notes (4 entities)
- **Rows on disk**: 14

## Methodology vintage

OpenStreetMap-derived plant inventory snapshot, community-curated; cross-referenced against CEA broad-strokes only

## Notes

v1 limitation: rollup is restricted to states already in datasets/reference/in/states.json with reference data (currently TN, KL, AS, WB). Of 35 states/UTs, only 4 render on this map; the other 31 appear grey because we lack data, not because they have zero capacity. v2 plan (see docs/research/energy-power-plants.md): switch upstream to CEA's monthly Installed Capacity report (PDF tabular extract), which gives state-wise totals for ALL states with proper methodology. Until then, treat this map as illustrative of what the schema can do, not as a credible state-comparison.
